level_signals: 2–4 years of experience in a product-related role
This role suits someone early in their PM career who loves to learn, collaborate, and build meaningful solutions

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2–4 years of experience in a product-related role (e.g. Product Manager, startup operator, founder, or product analyst).
Proven track record of building or launching products end-to-end.
Experience collaborating closely with engineering and design teams to deliver value quickly.
Excellent written and verbal communication; able to explain complex ideas simply and effectively
Comfortable operating in fast-moving, ambiguous environments.
Familiarity using AI in your day-to-day product work, such as prototyping, research, or analysis.
Experience in an early-stage or high-growth startup environment.

scope.surface: Product area within Plaid's open finance network (connecting, moving, and managing money)
scope.business_model: B2B2C — developer-facing API platform enabling financial account connectivity
core_responsibilities: Own a product area: define problems, write clear requirements, and drive execution with cross-functional teams.
Shape the roadmap using data, feedback, and market signals.
Collaborate with Engineering and Design to deliver intuitive, high-quality experiences.
Use metrics and user insights to measure and improve outcomes.
Communicate clearly, align stakeholders, and guide decisions.
Act with a founder mindset–simplify, move fast, and embrace feedback.
Learn continuously and help raise the bar for Plaid's products and culture.

green_flags: Explicitly encourages applications from candidates who don't fully match the JD — inclusive hiring stance
Clear seniority signal: 'early in their PM career', making level expectations transparent
Well-known brand (Plaid) with established product and scale — strong resume signal
Specific nice-to-have called out in brackets, making prioritization transparent
red_flags: Location/remote policy not stated in JD body — city (SF HQ) inferred from company description, not role-specific
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Role scope is broad and unspecific — no product area named, making it hard to assess fit

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from '2–4 years' and 'early in their PM career' language)
location.cities_listed (inferred from HQ description in intro, not from a role-specific location field)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated in JD
ambiguities: The startup experience requirement ('Experience in an early-stage or high-growth startup environment') appears in the Qualifications section without a 'Nice to Have' tag, but is listed alongside other unlabeled bullets that are clearly expected — treated as hard. However, the boundary between required and preferred for this bullet is ambiguous given Plaid itself is a late-stage company.
Location policy is entirely absent from the JD body. San Francisco is mentioned only as HQ in the company description. Remote eligibility and office requirements are unknown.
